metadata.default_description: A respectful digital memorial linked to a physical QR plaque. Each page reads the verses of Psalm 119 that match the deceased's Hebrew name.

hero.subtitle: A small QR code on the plaque opens a private page where the family shares a biography, photographs, and a reading of Psalm 119 generated from the deceased's Hebrew name — one section per letter, in keeping with tradition.

tradition.title: Twenty-two sections, one for each letter.
tradition.p1_html: Psalm 119 is the longest chapter in the Hebrew Bible: 176 verses, divided into 22 sections of 8 verses each. Every section opens with a successive letter of the Hebrew alphabet — an acrostic that has been studied, sung, and recited for thousands of years.
tradition.p2_html: In the memory of a loved one, the tradition is to read the sections whose initial letters spell their Hebrew name, then the four sections of נשמה (neshamah, "soul"). It is a way of speaking the person's name in sacred verse.
tradition.example_explanation_html: The dark squares are the letters whose sections are read aloud: the four letters of ראובן (Reuven), then the four letters of נשמה (neshamah).

how_it_works.title: Three steps. Nothing else to learn.
how_it_works.step1_body: We provide a small, dignified QR plaque designed to be added to the headstone or memorial monument. Built to last, easy to scan.
how_it_works.step2_body: The QR opens a calm, ad-free page that the family controls. Biography, photographs, both civil and Hebrew dates, and the corresponding Psalm 119 reading.
how_it_works.step3_body: We give the family a private edit link. They can add photos and update the biography over the years. We are here for any change to the Hebrew name itself.

whats_on.title: Everything that matters, nothing that doesn't.
whats_on.subtitle: No advertisements, no recommendations, no related profiles. Each page is built around one person, the way it should be.
items: Civil and Hebrew names, displayed with proper typography
Both civil and Hebrew dates of passing
A biography written by the family, in their own words
A gallery of photographs the family chooses
The Psalm 119 reading, drawn from the Hebrew name
A private edit link reserved for the family

items.title: No advertising. Ever.
items.body: A memorial is not a marketing surface. We will never run ads or sponsored content on these pages.

items.title: No tracking pixels.
items.body: Visitors are not profiled, retargeted, or sold. The page loads, and that is the entire transaction.

items.title: Respect for tradition.
items.body: Hebrew names and Psalm 119 verses are rendered with care — niqqud, te'amim, sof pasuq. We verify spellings before changing them.

items.title: Your data, kept private.
items.body: Family-edit links are unguessable and revocable. Pages are unlisted unless you ask us to publish them.

contact.title_line1: A memorial for a loved one,
contact.body: We work with families, rabbis, and funeral homes. Tell us a little about who you are remembering and we'll handle the rest — the Hebrew spelling, the page, the QR plaque.

not_found.title: Memorial not found
not_found.body: This memorial doesn't exist or hasn't been published yet. If you scanned a QR code from a plaque, the family may still be preparing the page.

family.intro_family: Anyone with this link can edit the memorial. Keep it private to your family.
family.intro_admin: You're editing as administrator. The Hebrew name is unlocked here — change it carefully, it drives the Psalm 119 reading.
family.invalid_link_title: This edit link is not valid.
family.invalid_link_body: Family edit links are unique and unguessable. Double-check the URL, or ask the administrator for a fresh link.
family.field_hebrew_name_help: Drives the Psalm 119 reading. Verify spelling carefully.
family.field_hebrew_name_locked_help: The Hebrew name determines the Psalm 119 reading. To correct it, please contact us — we verify the spelling carefully before changing it.
family.field_hebrew_name_empty: Not set yet — please contact us.
family.biography_placeholder: A few paragraphs about your loved one — separate paragraphs with a blank line.
family.photos_help: Up to 10 MB per image. You can pick several at once.
family.photo_too_large: "{name}" is over 10 MB ({size}). Pick a smaller file or compress it.
family.no_photos: No photos yet. Add some above.
family.uploading_warning: Large photos can take a moment. Don't close this tab.

admin.title: EternaLink memorials
admin.subtitle: Create new memorials and manage publication. The family-edit link is unguessable and acts as the credential — share it only with the family.

create.title: New memorial
create.subtitle: Generate a slug and a private family-edit link. You can fill the biography and photos later from the family link.

created_notice.label: Family edit link
created_notice.title: Share this private link with the family
created_notice.warning: This link is the only way to edit the memorial. Any previous link has been invalidated. There is no password recovery — keep it safe.

list.title: Memorials
list.empty: No memorials yet. Create the first one above.
list.regenerate_confirm:
Generate a new family-edit link?

The current link will stop working immediately. Make sure you can share the new one with the family.
list.delete_confirm:
Permanently delete "{name}"?

This cannot be undone. The memorial page, the family-edit link, and all uploaded photos will be removed.

Type DELETE to confirm:

login.title: Admin sign in
login.auth_misconfigured: Auth not configured on the server.

errors.label: Something went wrong
errors.title: We couldn't load this page.
errors.body_too_large: Photos must be under 10 MB. Compress the image (most phones have a built-in setting) and try again.

setup.label: Setup required
setup.supabase_title: EternaLink isn't connected to Supabase yet
setup.supabase_body: {context} needs a Supabase project. Follow the SUPABASE_SETUP.md guide in the repository root, then add the values to .env.local and restart the dev server.
setup.admin_password_body: Set ADMIN_PASSWORD in .env.local (see SUPABASE_SETUP.md) and restart the dev server.
